summary: Frontend testing completed for offline sync fix. 6 of 7 features tested PASSED. 1 CRITICAL BUG found: Gestión de Predios de Actualización page is stuck in 'Cargando predios...' loading state indefinitely even though the API returns 200 OK and data is saved to IndexedDB.

ui_bugs.issue: Predios list stuck in 'Cargando predios...' loading state indefinitely. Statistics show correctly (3234 Total, 3220 Pendientes, 10 Visitados, 4 Actualizados, 501 Con Mejoras) but the predios list never renders. The loadingPredios state is not being set to false after fetchPredios completes.
evidence: Page shows 'Mostrando 50 de 3234 predios' but list is stuck on loading
ui_bugs.root_cause_hypothesis: The fetchPredios useCallback has dependencies on isOnline, getPrediosOffline, downloadForOffline, and proyecto?.municipio. When any of these change, fetchPredios is recreated, which triggers the useEffect again. This may cause a race condition where loadingPredios is set to true by a new call before the previous call's finally block executes.

action_items: FIX CRITICAL: GestionPrediosActualizacion.js - loadingPredios state not being set to false after fetchPredios completes. The predios list is stuck in loading state indefinitely.
Investigate race condition in fetchPredios useCallback - dependencies may be causing multiple calls
Consider adding a loading timeout or error handling to prevent infinite loading state
critical_code_review_comments: GestionPrediosActualizacion.js line 605: fetchPredios useCallback has many dependencies that may cause re-creation and race conditions
GestionPrediosActualizacion.js line 607-611: useEffect depends on fetchPredios which changes when its dependencies change
The finally block at line 601-604 should always execute, but the loading state is not being updated - possible async race condition
context_for_next_testing_agent: Gestión de Predios de Actualización page has a critical bug where predios list is stuck in loading state. All other pages (Login, Dashboard, Proyectos de Actualización, Visor de Actualización, Gestión de Predios Conservación, Visor de Predios Conservación) are working correctly.

Statistics: Total: 3234, Pendientes: 3220, Visitados: 10, Actualizados: 4, Mejoras: 1969, Const. ✓ 6912. Map shows geometries (green for rural, blue for urban). 6076 geometrías descargadas - Listo para trabajo offline.

Statistics show correctly (3234 Total, 3220 Pendientes, 10 Visitados, 4 Actualizados, 501 Con Mejoras). Shows 'Mostrando 50 de 3234 predios'. BUT predios list is stuck on 'Cargando predios...' indefinitely. Waited 35+ seconds and still loading.

bug_details.symptom: Predios list stuck in loading state

6_gestion_predios_conservacion.details: Page loads correctly at /dashboard/predios. Shows Total Predios: 58,810, Avalúo Total: $ 3.636.403.257.598, Área R1: 990.600 ha 7.987,15 m², Base Gráfica: 19,443. Shows Predios por Municipio with all municipalities and their counts.

7_visor_predios_conservacion.details: Map loads correctly at /dashboard/visor-predios. Shows 19,688 predios en Base Gráfica (Rural: 12,901, Urbano: 6,787). Map shows municipality boundaries. Estado GDB: 2 cargados, 10 pendientes.

secondary_db_issue: [useOffline] Found 0 predios in secondary DB - This message appears multiple times, indicating the secondary DB (useOffline.js) is not synced with the primary DB (offlineDB.js)
console_logs_analysis.no_errors: No JavaScript errors or exceptions found in console

rca_of_the_issue.bug: Gestión de Predios de Actualización - Predios list stuck in loading state
reproduction_steps: 4. Observe that statistics are displayed but predios list shows 'Cargando predios...' indefinitely
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The fetchPredios useCallback in GestionPrediosActualizacion.js has dependencies that may cause it to be recreated multiple times during component mount. This can lead to a race condition where the loadingPredios state is set to true by a new call before the previous call's finally block executes, leaving the UI in a permanent loading state.
mitigation: Add a ref to track if a fetch is in progress and skip new calls if one is already running
Use a stable reference for the fetch function instead of recreating it on every dependency change
Add a timeout to automatically set loadingPredios to false if it takes too long
Consider using useRef to track the mounted state and prevent state updates after unmount
